title: silene velutina subsp. andryalifolia
intro: Silene velutina subsp. andryalifolia, also known as Andry's catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Andry's catchfly include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Andry's catchfly can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, with the tea being a popular method of consumption.
